title: Front-end Developer Jr
heroTitle: Front-end Developer Jr focused on performance and accessibility
heroSub: I build clear, responsive, production-ready interfaces with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and React.
aboutText: I am a Junior Front-end Developer and I turn requirements into reusable, accessible, and maintainable interfaces. I have hands-on practice with real study projects, Git/GitHub version control, and incremental delivery with clear team communication.
projectsSubtitle: Each project summarizes Problem, Solution, and Outcome for faster technical evaluation.
project1Desc: Problem: manual conversion was slow. Solution: API-based SPA with input validation. Outcome: faster, safer conversion flow.
project1Detail1: Problem: users faced delay and input errors during manual currency conversion.
project1Detail2: Solution: integrated exchange-rate API and real-time input validation.
project1Detail3: Outcome: faster flow, fewer mistakes, and clearer value formatting.
project2Desc: Problem: low engagement in logic practice. Solution: instant-feedback game loop. Outcome: interactive and educational experience.
project2Detail1: Problem: static exercises did not keep users engaged while learning logic.
project2Detail2: Solution: attempt tracking, comparison rules, and instant feedback per guess.
project2Detail3: Outcome: continuous interaction and stronger logic practice through gameplay.
project3Desc: Problem: scattered content. Solution: responsive layout with visual hierarchy. Outcome: smoother reading on desktop and mobile.
project3Detail1: Problem: original page lacked hierarchy to guide reading.
project3Detail2: Solution: semantic structure and responsive section-based layout.
project3Detail3: Outcome: more predictable navigation and better scanability across screens.
project4Desc: Problem: weak service communication. Solution: CTA-driven landing page. Outcome: clearer user journey to contact.
project4Detail1: Problem: value proposition was not clear enough for potential clients.
project4Detail2: Solution: CTA-focused landing page with direct sections and mobile priority.
project4Detail3: Outcome: simpler contact journey and improved service understanding.
project5Desc: Problem: long page with weak structure. Solution: modular sections and visual consistency. Outcome: easier scanning and navigation.
project5Detail1: Problem: too much content without an efficient visual breakdown.
project5Detail2: Solution: modular content blocks with hierarchy, imagery, and balanced typography.
project5Detail3: Outcome: more comfortable reading and easier long-term maintenance.
project6Desc: Problem: music portfolio needed a stronger identity. Solution: minimalist visual direction. Outcome: faster discovery of published work.
project6Detail1: Problem: portfolio needed to reinforce personal branding without visual noise.
project6Detail2: Solution: minimalist interface focused on quick navigation and content prominence.
project6Detail3: Outcome: smoother experience and better discovery of available beats.
processStep1Text: I define the problem, project goals, and target audience to guide technical decisions from the start.
processStep2Text: I implement responsive structure, reusable components, and clean code.
processStep3Text: I validate accessibility, performance, and visual details for professional delivery.
curiositiesText: I produce music in my free time and apply the same rhythm, consistency, and detail.
curiositiesSkill: This habit improves my attention to rhythm, detail, and consistency in interface design.
contactLead: Open to Junior Front-end opportunities in remote or hybrid format.
